title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Inspector Corrado and Chief Inspector Ridestad discuss the possibility of Letitia Blacklock moving to a remote location in Scotland to stay with Bella Goddard for her safety. They debate the logistics of committing a murder, noting that a killer must act quickly if a target is already dying. During their discussion, Ridestad receives a call from Detective Inspector Ridge of Chipping Cleggford, who informs him that Dora Bunner has died. Although she appeared to have taken aspirin from Letitia’s bedside table, a pathologist determines she died of an anesthetic overdose. Ridestad notes that because many people attended Miss Bunner's birthday party the previous day, anyone could have switched the pills.

At the vicarage, Miss Marple delivers a note from the vicar, Julian, to Letitia. The note explains that Julian is attending to a dying parishioner and suggests a funeral for Wednesday. It mentions that "poor old Bonnie" accidentally took the poisoned aspirin intended for someone else. While waiting, Miss Marple reflects on Patrick Simmons's potential involvement in a robbery involving a pair of Dresden ware lamps—a shepherd boy and a shepherdess. She recalls Miss Bunner's claim that Patrick had tampered with the lamps, suggesting they may have been switched to facilitate a crime. When Letitia arrives, she reads the note and breaks down in intense grief, explaining that Dora Bunner was her only link to the past. Miss Marple offers sympathy as Letitia prepares a reply to the clergyman.
